items.title: US court overturns US$16.1bil judgment against Argentina over oil firm seizure
items.summary: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina: A US appeals court on Friday overturned a US$16.1 billion judgment against Argentina for nationalising the oil company YPF in 2012. "We won the YPF trial," Milei wrote in capital letters on the social media platform X, calling the 2-1 ruling by the 2nd US Circuit Court of Appeals in New York the "best possible outcome." The court struck down a 2023 ruling from Judge Loretta Preska of the US District Court for the Southern District of New York that ordered Argentina to pay US$16.1 billion to minority shareholder companies she said were harmed by the nationalisation of YPF.

items.title: Rubio urges world to stop Iran imposing tolls in key strait
items.summary: PARIS: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday he had made progress with allies in opposing Iranian threats to start "tolling" vessels that pass through the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Rubio, after attending talks of the Group of Seven powers, said he had voiced concern that Iran may impose a permanent tolling system "immediately after" the war ends. He said he had found "a lot of buy-in" to opposing any Iranian tolling in the strait.

items.title: Genting restaurant under probe over RM902 patin dish
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A restaurant in Genting Highlands is under investigation following public uproar over a RM902 steamed patin (silver catfish) dish. Sinchew reported that the Pahang branch of the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry conducted checks on Loong Kee Restaurant on Thursday, confirming that the price was clearly stated on the menu at RM33.80 per 100g. Authorities have issued a notice requiring the restaurant to submit detailed cost and pricing information within four business days to determine whether profiteering is involved.

items.title: Driver's spatial awareness questioned after 'avoidable' crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A dashcam recording of a minor accident has gone viral, showing a red car turning onto a main road and colliding with another vehicle despite ample space to pass. In the footage, two vehicles can be seen exiting a petrol station and turning right onto the main road. An oncoming car in the left lane, from which the dashcam footage was filmed, slows down to give them plenty of space.

items.title: GOF arrests three Thai men for attempting to smuggle petrol, diesel into Thailand
items.summary: KANGAR: The General Operations Force (GOF) detained three Thai men who were attempting to smuggle petrol and diesel into Thailand using modified vehicle tanks on Jalan Kangar-Padang Besar, near the Padang Besar Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security Complex (ICQS) on Thursday. Its Northern Brigade Commander, SAC Balveer Singh Mahindar Singh said the police arrested the men, aged between 22 and 45, after inspecting three pickup trucks during 'Op Taring Bravo 1' at 9am. – Bernama TAG: Perlis, petrol, diesel, seized, tanks, smuggle, GOF, Padang Besar, ICQS, Kangar, Thai men, police, arrested, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, traffic, Op Taring, Malaysia, news, NST, region © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: PKR orders two leaders to step down over 'protection money' claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR has instructed two leaders linked to a viral video controversy to immediately relinquish their positions in the Federal Territories Residents' Representative Council. Federal Territories PKR leadership council chairman Datuk Azman Abidin said its political bureau said the directive was issued to R. Vijeyndran and G. Paarthiydasan, who are alleged to have solicited "protection money" from a business premises in Wangsa Maju. He said the move was necessary to allow police investigations and internal party probes to proceed without interference.

items.title: China is challenging US spaceflight supremacy
items.summary: Walking on the Moon by 2030, building a lunar base, and then perhaps on to Mars: after 30 years of honing its expertise, China is challenging the United States' supremacy in spaceflight. As US space agency Nasa prepares for its crewed lunar flyby mission, Artemis II, let's take a look at China's ambitions in space: China's crewed space programme —known as Project 921 —was launched on Sept 21, 1992, aiming to develop its own expertise and space station. Blocked from the International Space Station (ISS) in 2011 by the US, China built its own orbital hub.

items.title: Hot spell sparks four major fires across Pahang, 1,000ha destroyed
items.summary: KUANTAN: The ongoing hot spell has sparked four major fire incidents across Pahang, including two at landfill sites, with more than 1,000ha of land already destroyed. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (operations) Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the fire in Nenasi, Pekan, which has affected about 735ha of a 2,000-hectare plantation , entered its sixth day today. "Twenty-seven firefighters from eight fire stations, including those from neighbouring districts, along with 10 plantation workers, have been divided into three teams to douse the fire," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Man arrested for slapping teen at LRT station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested an unemployed man in connection with an incident in which a teenage boy was slapped while purchasing bread at a shop in the Pasar Seni LRT station here earlier this week. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said the incident occurred at a Rotiboy outlet at about 1.30pm on March 24. Following a report, policemen conducted investigations and arrested a 47-year-old local man by the roadside along Jalan Masjid India at about 3.40pm on March 27.

items.title: From ramen to toys, shortages hit Asian manufacturers
items.summary: From beer and crisps to noodles, toys and cosmetics, companies —and consumers —across Asia are bracing for a crisis as the Iran war wreaks havoc on supply chains, plastics and oil supplies, upending everyday life and sending prices soaring. Choi Gun-soo, the manager of a 57-year-old South Korean factory that makes plastic films used by farmers to cover crops as well as by television manufacturers, said his suppliers were raising prices of some raw materials as much as 50 per cent, while other suppliers had simply run out of stock. While they had weathered past oil shocks as well as the Covid-19 pandemic, the impact from the war was unprecedented, said Choi, adding that the company had cut production to only 20 per cent to 30 per cent of usual output.

items.title: Report: Eight Israelis detained at KLIA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: At least eight Israeli citizens were detained at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) in recent months, according to a report by Israeli news outlet Ynet. The report claimed that some of those detained held dual nationality, while others were travelling solely on Israeli passports. Hazan warned Israelis against travelling to Malaysia.

items.title: NST Leader: How to be prudent during times of crisis
items.summary: Recent fluctuations in food and fuel prices are not just isolated economic inconveniences, but clear signals for the public to adopt a more sophisticated understanding of supply chains and personal resource management. Take the projected rise in vegetable prices caused by the nexus of local drought conditions and geopolitical instability (Middle East conflict). Farmers' associations are already warning that prices may surge by 50 per cent before settling at 30 per cent.

items.title: Police rescue 14, arrest 7 in Melaka human trafficking raids
items.summary: MELAKA: Police detained seven people for alleged involvement in human trafficking and rescued 14 others believed to be victims of forced labour here today. Dzulkhairi said that in the same operation, police also rescued 14 victims aged between 25 and 36, comprising seven men and six women from the Philippines, as well as one woman from Myanmar. He said initial investigations found that all the victims were believed to have been trafficked for forced labour exploitation in online scam activities.

items.title: Container falls off speeding trailer, causes traffic snarl in Port Klang [WATCH]
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Traffic was congested along Jalan Kem in Port Klang, near here, today after a container fell off a trailer believed to have been speeding. South Klang district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about noon, did not result in any casualties or damage to other vehicles. "Initial investigations found that the trailer was believed to be speeding when passing through the location, causing the container to become detached from the trailer before falling onto the road.

items.title: Anwar concludes Jakarta visit after meeting with Prabowo
items.summary: JAKARTA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim concluded his special visit to Jakarta today after meeting Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto at the Istana Merdeka here. Following the discussions, Prabowo accompanied Anwar to Halim Perdanakusuma Airport as a gesture of goodwill and cordial ties between the two leaders. The visit was undertaken at Prabowo's invitation following a phone conversation between the two leaders on March 23, and marked Anwar's first trip to Jakarta this year.

items.title: Philippines shifts preparatory meetings for 2026 Asean chairmanship to virtual formats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Philippines has shifted preparatory meetings for its 2026 Asean Chairmanship to virtual formats, citing developments in Middle East and their impact on regional priorities, particularly energy security. As part of this directive, approximately 650 preparatory meetings will now be conducted online, a press statement posted on the Chair's official website said Friday. The Summit is expected to tackle urgent priorities, including energy security, food security, and migrant workers' concerns.

items.title: Youth groups, parties slam Philippine senator over call to 'revive' Sabah claim amid fuel crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Political parties and youth groups have slammed a Philippine senator for calling to revive Manila's claim over Sabah, calling it "irresponsible and provocative." She said the attempt to link Sabah's sovereignty with its petroleum resources was particularly concerning, suggesting that such narratives reflect efforts to politicise territorial issues for narrow economic interests amid the ongoing global fuel crisis. This comes after Senator Robin Padilla urged President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to revive the Philippines' longstanding claim over Sabah, citing the territory's rich oil reserves amid the ongoing global fuel crisis.

items.title: MOH relaunches HSOP to speed up MRI services at HKL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry (MOH) has reintroduced the Hospital Services Outsourcing Programme (HSOP) at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) to ease congestion and reduce waiting times for patients requiring magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) services. In a statement today, HKL said the HSOP reduced patient waiting times by 33 per cent in 2025, from 24 weeks to 16 weeks. "For this year, the hospital has applied for 640 slots, a 70 per cent increase, to shorten patient waiting times even more," read the statement.

items.title: Fisherman killed in crash hours before engagement
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: Twenty-five-year-old Ain Sofea Aminu Nansar had hoped to be engaged to her partner, 33-year-old Mohamad Shahrin Mohd, by the end of today. Ain said she never imagined that their final meeting last night would become their last memory before receiving the devastating news. But I never imagined he would not live to wear it," she said.

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - March 27, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: RON95 subsidy extended Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government will explore all options to sustain the RON95 subsidy beyond May. Hormuz talks ongoing Iran says it is engaging "intimate friends" including Malaysia to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions. School closure rule Deputy minister Wong Kah Woh said schools may close if temperatures exceed 37°C for three consecutive days.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Friday, March 27, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1404994]] [[nid:1404993]] [[nid:1404992]] [[nid:1404991]] [[nid:1404990]] [[nid:1404989]] [[nid:1404988]] [[nid:1404987]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Body of teenage boy found after suspected crocodile attack
items.summary: KUCHING: The body of a teenage boy who was suspected to have been a victim of a crocodile attack while bathing with his family along Sungai Sarawak in Kampung Segubang, Batu Kawa, was found this afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Operations Centre said that the body of the boy, 15, was found by family members during a search and rescue operation at about 5.30pm not far from where he went missing. "The body of the victim was found without his left and right arms," the centre said, adding that the body has been handed over to the police for further action.

items.title: Dr M calls for Malay unity in first political post after return
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad today said the ability to unite Malays outweighs the importance of holding positions such as party president or prime ministerial candidate. He said there was little value in holding positions such as party president or prime ministerial candidate if divisions persist, noting that fragmentation into multiple parties would only weaken electoral prospects. "Yet some leaders still believe that to become prime minister, one must first be the head of a party.

items.title: Turning food waste into energy: Malaysia's buffer against geopolitical shocks
items.summary: LETTERS: As geopolitical tensions, particularly the escalating United States–Iran conflict, continue to disrupt global energy markets, Malaysia is facing mounting pressure from rising oil prices and supply uncertainties. For Malaysia, the impact is immediate: higher fuel import costs, increased electricity generation expenses, and growing fiscal pressure on government subsidies. At the same time, natural gas prices have also risen by 20–30 per cent in regional spot markets, further straining Malaysia's gas-dependent power sector.

items.title: Austria to ban social media for children under 15 from 2027
items.summary: VIENNA: The Austrian federal government announced on Friday plans to ban social media use for students aged 14 and under, according to the Austrian news agency APA , reported Xinhua. Beginning in the 2027/28 school year, the reform will offer students increased instruction in media literacy, democracy, and the use of artificial intelligence (AI). Meanwhile, the new compulsory subject, "Media and Democracy," will highlight the value of traditional and social media.

items.title: Ringgit softens against US dollar amid Brent crude gains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit ended lower against the US dollar and other major currencies on Friday as Brent crude oil prices gained traction amid uncertainties over the US-Israel war on Iran, said an economist. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said US President Donald Trump's 10-day deadline extension for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz failed to calm the crude oil market. The local currency also traded lower or flat against Asean currencies.

items.title: Perhilitan clarifies 19 elephants on loan for conservation, not sale
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The allegation that the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) sold 19 elephants to certain parties, as circulated on social media, is false and misleading. Perhilitan director-general (DG) Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said the elephants in question were not sold but instead are being transferred on loan for ongoing wildlife conservation and management. He said this collaboration also opens up opportunities for technology and expertise transfer, particularly in the fields of controlled breeding, health monitoring, nutrition and modern habitat management.

items.title: Penang considers WFH for civil servants, waits for word from Putrajaya
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — The Penang state government is studying the implementation of working from home (WFH) for civil servants following challenges arising from rising fuel prices, particularly diesel, amid the West Asia crisis. He said the implementation of WFH is a measure to reduce the burden of operating costs and daily travel following the increase in petrol and diesel prices. Chow said the rise in diesel prices also has an indirect impact on the government sector, including local authorities in Penang, such as solid waste management services that depend entirely on diesel.

items.title: Father escapes, four-year-old burnt to death in Volvo lorry fire in Pasir Gudang
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 27 — A four-year-old boy was burnt to death after the lorry he was travelling in with an adult man, believed to be his father, caught fire at Iskandar Halal Park, Cahaya Baru, Pasir Gudang, here this morning. Pasir Gudang Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Iszahwati Supardi said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 10.46 am, and six personnel with a Fire Rescue Tender were dispatched to the scene. Firefighters subsequently extinguished the fire.

items.title: Luno lists 29 new digital assets, bringing total to 51
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Luno has launched 29 new digital assets, its largest batch to date, bringing the total number of cryptocurrencies on its platform to 51. The company said this marks the widest selection of crypto assets available on any regulated platform in Malaysia. The newly approved assets span eight categories, namely Layer 1, Layer 2, decentralised finance (DeFi), artificial intelligence and crypto, gaming, non-fungible tokens (NFTs), real-world assets and decentralised physical infrastructure networks (DePIN).

items.title: Bursa Malaysia, HKEX ink pact on dual listings, indices
items.summary: The partnership aims to strengthen capital market connectivity between Malaysia and Hong Kong, covering areas such as exchange-traded funds (ETFs), Syariah-compliant securities and the carbon market. As part of the collaboration, the two exchanges launched the HKEX Bursa Malaysia Large Cap Index, a co-branded benchmark designed to improve cross-market access for investors. Bursa Malaysia chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Mohamed said the index offers a single, investable benchmark comprising leading large-cap companies from both markets, reflecting their scale, sector diversity and strengths.

items.title: iCents wins RM14.1mil data centre contract in Indonesia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: iCents Group Holdings Bhd's unit Maytech Cleanroom Manufacturing Sdn Bhd has secured a RM14.1 million contract for a data centre project in Indonesia. Managing director Vincent Ong Mum Fei said the award reflects the group's continued participation in data centre-related projects, including those outside Malaysia. iCents said it will continue to strengthen its project execution capabilities and expand its presence in high-specification industries, including data centre developments.

items.title: ASNB declares RM2.36bil distribution for ASB 2, ASM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (ASNB) has declared a total income distribution of RM2.36 billion for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, for its two fixed-price funds, Amanah Saham Bumiputera 2 (ASB 2) and Amanah Saham Malaysia (ASM). ASB 2 declared a total income distribution of RM820 million, equivalent to 5.50 sen per unit for 680,141 unitholders, while ASM declared RM1.54 billion, equivalent to five sen per unit for 786,088 unitholders. As of March 24, ASB 2 recorded net realised income of RM900 million, while ASM posted RM1.64 billion.

items.title: Viral video sparks KL police probe into alleged extortion by FT reps in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Police have received three reports over a viral video showing two individuals, suspected to be members of the Federal Territories Residents’ Representative Council (MPP), allegedly soliciting protection money from a massage centre in Wangsa Maju. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the case is being investigated under Section 385 of the Penal Code for extortion, as well as Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998 related to the dissemination of content. Recently, closed-circuit television footage went viral showing two men introducing themselves as MPP members while asking for money from a massage centre.

items.title: Perlis, parts of Kedah and Perak hit heatwave Level 2, 13 other areas in peninsula at Level 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The whole of Perlis, along with four districts in Kedah and one in Perak, are at heatwave level 2, while 13 other areas in peninsular Malaysia are at level 1. The 13 areas at heatwave level 1 are Padang Terap, Kubang Pasu, Pulau Langkawi, Kuala Muda, Sik, Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah, Timur Laut and Utara (Penang), as well as Selama, Kinta, Kuala Kangsar and Perak Tengah (Perak). A heatwave level 2 is declared when the maximum daily temperature is between 37° to 40° Celsius for a period of at least three consecutive days, while heatwave level 1 is a maximum daily temperature of 35° to 37°Celsius Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Malaysia’s medicine stock stable for now, but govt’s main supplier sets up task force over disruption risk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysia’s medicine supply remains stable for the next few months, even as global trade disruptions caused by the conflict in West Asia threaten pharmaceutical supply chains worldwide. He added that the situation underscores the need to strengthen Malaysia’s domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing capabilities to ensure long-term medicine security. Zulkifli also emphasised the importance of enhancing Malaysia’s domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing capabilities.

items.title: MACC seeks whistleblowers as RM10m bribery claims involving politician swirl
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has urged any party with credible information regarding the alleged soliciting and paying of money to a politician to come forward and lodge an official report. In a statement today, the MACC said an official report must be made to enable a thorough, transparent and fact-based investigation to be carried out. “The MACC also takes note of various allegations circulating in the media (regarding this case) and to date, no official report has been received by the MACC in relation to these claims, and the information that has gone viral has yet to be verified,” it said.

items.title: Malaysian Armed Forces expands use of drones in fight against fuel smuggling at borders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) is strengthening operational readiness by deploying assets and resources comprehensively in all border areas of the country to combat the diversion and leakage of fuel subsidies. The MAF also strengthens strategic cooperation with various enforcement agencies through real-time information sharing and the implementation of integrated operations, including intensified dynamic patrols in border areas and fuel distribution points. The cooperation involves the Royal Malaysia Police, the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency, the Royal Malaysian Customs Department, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency and other security agencies.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Indonesia for talks with Prabowo on Middle East crisis and bilateral ties
items.summary: JAKARTA, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Jakarta on Friday afternoon for a special visit to discuss escalating geopolitical developments in West Asia and strengthen Malaysia-Indonesia bilateral cooperation. The aircraft carrying Anwar landed at Halim Perdanakusuma Airport in East Jakarta at 3.45pm local time. The Prime Minister was received by Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono, Indonesia’s Ambassador to Malaysia Raden Datuk Mohammad Iman Hascarya and Malaysia’s Ambassador to Indonesia Datuk Syed Mohamad Hasrin Tengku Hussin.

items.title: English text of Federal Constitution prevails until Agong says otherwise, Putrajaya tells courts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The English version of the Federal Constitution is the nation’s authoritative legal document until the Yang di-Pertuan Agong declares otherwise, Putrajaya has asserted in its official defence against a lawsuit seeking to elevate the Bahasa Melayu version. It cited two landmark Federal Court rulings in the cases of Indira Gandhi and Loh Siew Hong as binding precedents that have already settled the matter. The lawsuit was filed last year by lawyer Haniff Khatri Abdulla, who is seeking a court declaration that the Bahasa Melayu version of the constitution should take precedence.

items.title: Karambunai state polls result stands as Election Court throws out Warisan challenge
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court on Friday struck out a Warisan candidate’s election petition seeking to nullify the Karambunai constituency election result in the 17th Sabah state election. High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, via Zoom, allowed all preliminary objections raised by the respondents to strike out the election petition filed against them. The petitioner, Ahmad Jais Otong, named the returning officer (RO) for the Karambunai state constituency election, the Election Commission (EC) of Malaysia and Perikatan Nasional’s PAS candidate, Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan, as the first, second and third respondents, respectively.

items.title: Driver dies after Isuzu D-Max plunges into drain in Sandakan crash
items.summary: The victim, identified as Chock Vui Heong, 50, was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel. Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the department received an emergency call at 12.56pm before a team was dispatched to the location about seven kilometres away. He said the victim was extricated using special equipment, including hydraulic rescue tools, but was confirmed dead at the scene before the body was sent to hospital, while operations ended at 1.58pm after ensuring the area was safe.

items.title: KK court fixes May 25 mention in Shafie Apdal contempt proceedings linked to Zara Qairina inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Sessions Court has fixed May 25 for a mention in the committal proceedings involving former chief minister Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal to receive an update on a judicial review application seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir. Shafie is alleged to have made remarks concerning the inquest that may amount to contempt of court, while the court had previously granted a stay of the committal proceedings pending updates on the judicial review. The court had earlier granted the Attorney General leave to commence contempt proceedings against Shafie and activist Che Muna Din, while a judicial review application filed by Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia challenging the inquest proceedings is opposed by the Attorney General’s Chambers, with respective counsel representing the parties.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues storm alert for parts of Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement that Kapit (Belaga) and Limbang in Sarawak are affected. Meanwhile, the Interior (Sipitang and Beaufort) and West Coast (Papar and Penampang) in Sabah are also affected.

items.title: Sibu man jailed four months for slashing girlfriend’s ex-husband with knife
items.summary: Romario also ordered for the sentence to run from the date of Maxwell’s conviction. However, upon arrival, the complainant got into an argument with Maxwell, who is said to be the boyfriend of the complainant’s ex-wife. During the altercation, Maxwell injured the complainant on the cheek using a sharp weapon.

items.title: Fire rips through 11 homes in Batu Ferringhi, residents escape unhurt
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — Residents of 11 houses in Kampung Din Manbo, Batu Ferringhi, here, went through anxious moments when their homes were destroyed in a fire this morning. Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis said 23 personnel with seven fire engines from the Teluk Bahang, Jalan Perak and Bayan Baru fire stations were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 11.29am. “The fire involved 11 double-storey semi-permanent houses (Class B), with structural damage estimated at about 80 per cent.

items.title: Education Ministry reinforces safety measures in schools after King’s heat warning
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) upholds the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to safeguard the safety and well-being of students, teachers and the school community, amid the current hot weather. Yesterday, in a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty emphasised the importance of safeguarding health by reducing outdoor activities exposed to hot and dry weather, and ensuring adequate water intake throughout the period. Sultan Ibrahim also said that the MOE should give serious attention to the matter, to ensure that the health of students is not adversely affected by the hot weather phenomenon.

items.title: Over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil seized in raid on Pasir Mas syndicate, says Kelantan Domestic Trade Ministry
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 27 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) busted a syndicate for misappropriating over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil in Kampung Lubuk Tapah, Pasir Mas, yesterday. “…the suspects were caught moving boxes of subsidised cooking oil outside a house thought to be a transit point before the supplies were sold on the black market,” he said in a statement today. He said 12 boxes of subsidised cooking oil (204kg) were found outside the house, with another 119kg inside, totalling 323kg seized.

items.title: Election Court strikes out Warisan petition over Pitas state seat, orders RM30,000 in costs
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court has struck out a petition filed by Warisan candidate Datuk Harun Ismail challenging the outcome of the Pitas state seat, which was won by Gabungan Rakyat Sabah’s Datuk Seri Ruddy Awah. Kuching-based High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, who sat as the election court judge via a Zoom proceeding, delivered the decision after allowing preliminary objections raised by the respondents. Harun, 66, filed the election petition on December 23 last year, naming Ruddy, 57, as the first respondent, Pitas returning officer as the second respondent, and the Election Commission as the third respondent.

items.title: Johor Bomba turns to aerial firefighting as winds reignite sections of 150-hectare fire
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 27 — An MI-17 helicopter of the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Air Unit will begin water bombing operations to extinguish the fire on Jalan Bypass Punggai-Sebana Cove, here. JBPM said the extinguishing operation is still continuing in section G by ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ using ‘water tankers’ to control the spread of the fire. For the fire near PPR Pengerang, 55 per cent or 5.5 hectares of the 10 hectares affected area were extinguished using the ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ methods in sections E, F and C, with water from nearby fire hydrants.

items.title: Merbok killings: Post-mortems done, couple’s bodies yet to be released pending forensic identification, say police
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 27 — Post-mortem examinations on the bodies of a married couple believed to have been murdered in an incident in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were completed yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said, however, both bodies have yet to be released to their family and are currently at the Forensic Unit of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar. It was reported yesterday that the bodies of three family members were discovered in a house in Kampung Belau, Merbok.

items.title: Malaysia eyes Petronas’ global investments for fuel supply buffer as US-Iran crisis worsens
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 27 — Malaysia is exploring ways to leverage Petronas’s diversified global investments in order to secure alternative supplies of fuel to stability supply in the country, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. “For now, supplies are still sufficient until May, but we are also seeking alternative options,” Anwar told reporters after Friday prayers here. Anwar reiterated that while current fuel supplies are assured until May, authorities are monitoring the volatile situation daily.

items.title: Sabah, Sarawak retain diesel subsidies due to geography, costs and reliance on fuel, minister says
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The federal government’s decision to maintain diesel subsidies for Sabah and Sarawak reflects the unique geography and infrastructure challenges faced by residents in both states. “The distinct geography, logistical costs and development challenges in Sabah and Sarawak are carefully considered in every national policy decision, particularly given the relatively high diesel usage in both states. He said that residents in Sabah and Sarawak will continue to enjoy subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre under controlled usage, alongside the Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) quota priced at RM1.99 per litre with a monthly cap of 200 litres.

items.title: Man charged with trafficking over 3kg of methamphetamine in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU, March 27 — A man, who is also a store worker, was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking more than 3kg of drugs earlier this month. Hadriansyah Sabran, 42, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The accused is charged with trafficking 3,071.04 grams of methamphetamine at a vehicle storage facility premises in Lorong Yong Luk, Jalan Sin Onn, here on March 13 at about 7.30pm.

items.title: Anwar convenes meeting of all state leaders over US-Iran crisis
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today that he will convene a meeting with all state leaders next week to ensure a coordinated national response to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. Speaking to reporters after Friday prayers here, Anwar said the meeting aims to brief the group on the measures being considered by the federal government, ensuring participation from all states, regardless of political affiliation. “God willing, next week I will convene all the menteri besar, the premier and the chief ministers to brief them so that all states, regardless of party affiliation, can participate together with the government, including the opposition,” he said.

items.title: Man on the run for four years charged with businessman’s murder in Mont Kiara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — An unemployed man who had been on the run for four years was brought before the Magistrate’s Court here today, on a charge of murdering a businessman in a residential area in Mont Kiara in 2022. VV Vadivelu, 39, along with three other individuals who had been charged earlier, is accused of murdering Goh Chok Chuan, 63, at a car park near the guardhouse of Amarin Kiara, Mont Kiara, here, at 11.40pm on July 11, 2022. The court set June 8 for mention.

items.title: Thirteen plead not guilty to rioting with weapons at Kuching eatery over alleged RM12,000 dispute
items.summary: KUCHING, March 27 — A total of 13 men claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court here to a charge of rioting while using objects capable of being used as weapons at an eatery along Jalan Kuching City Mall. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Aidil Akmal Sharidan sought bail of RM5,000 with one local surety for each accused to ensure their attendance at all court proceedings. Abdurrahman granted bail of RM3,500, undeposited, with two local sureties for Lee’s clients, while the remaining three accused, who were unrepresented, were allowed bail of RM3,500 deposited with one local surety.

items.title: Employers urged to adopt flexible work strategy as energy costs rise, says HR Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Ministry of Human Resources Malaysia (Kesuma) has called on employers to implement Flexible Work Arrangements (FWA) as a strategic measure to strengthen organisational resilience, reduce the impact of rising energy costs, and support sustainable economic growth. In a statement today, the ministry encouraged private sector employers to assess the suitability of adopting FWA based on operational needs, including through work-from-home (WFH) approaches, flexible working hours, job rotation or hybrid models. “Kesuma believes that implementing FWA offers comprehensive benefits to both employers and employees, particularly in the context of rising energy costs driven by geopolitical tensions and the reliance on daily commuting,” the statement said.

items.title: Schools in Malaysia may close if temperatures exceed 37°C for three days in a row, says deputy education minister
items.summary: IPOH, March 27 — School administrators may temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37°C for three consecutive days. “State Education Departments and schools have sufficient experience in managing the current situation. “Outdoor school activities must also be suspended if temperatures range between 35 and 37°C for three consecutive days, including cross-country runs, camping, drills and other outdoor activities,” he said.

items.title: Billions for nuclear? Charles Santiago says betting big on solar is better for Malaysia’s future
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 27 — Former Klang MP Charles Santiago today called on Malaysia to set a more ambitious renewable energy (RE) goal of at least 50 per cent by 2030, arguing that the current national target of 35 per cent is insufficient and leaves the nation vulnerable to global energy shocks. Speaking at a media conference on the impact of geopolitical conflict on climate change, Santiago, who is a former chairman of the National Water Services Commission (Span), said that while Malaysia has made progress, its continued reliance on fossil fuels is a risk amid rising tensions in the Middle East. “Given the ongoing geopolitical tensions and the need for greater energy independence, increasing domestic renewable energy capacity is essential for long-term resilience and sustainability,” he stated.
